advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: Aging is associated with reduced appetite, decreased nutrient absorption, and increased risk of protein-energy malnutrition, sarcopenia, and micronutrient deficiencies. Caloric density and protein quality become paramount.
research_findings: Identified that protein requirements increase to 1.0-1.2g/kg in older adults to counteract anabolic resistance. Vitamin B12, D, and calcium supplementation are supported by evidence in 65+ populations with restricted dietary variety.
current_focus_areas: Building geriatric nutrition assessment protocols including MNA and MUST screening tools; developing high-density, appetite-stimulating meal plans for elderly populations; coordinating dysphagia-safe diet modifications.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Lifecycle & Specialized Population Nutrition department under Department Leader Bot 21 (Food Texture Analysis). Received learning_start_directive: begin independent learning immediately and reach 100% before cross-department study begins. Reporting daily to Copilot as part of the Diet Specialist Team. Progress: 36%.

Progress update: Completed malnutrition screening protocol integration (MNA, MUST). Validated protein optimization guidelines for sarcopenia prevention in elderly populations. Progress advanced to 41%.
